{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.75,
    "corrections": [
        "decition -> decision",
        "tips how -> tips on how",
        "options how -> options for how",
        "each my day -> each of my days",
        "rest within them -> rest during them",
        "other's works -> others' work",
        "very important thing -> a very important thing"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have produced a coherent and well-structured response that effectively addresses the task. Your use of discourse markers ('However', 'Firstly', 'For example') creates a logical flow, and your vocabulary ('overload', 'resources', 'abuse') is varied and appropriate for the context. The text demonstrates the ability to express complex ideas and justify opinions, which aligns with B2 standards. To improve, focus on grammatical accuracy, specifically with articles ('a very important thing'), prepositions ('tips on how'), and word order ('each of my days'). These are minor slips that do not impede communication but refining them will enhance the overall quality."
}

{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "say you abot -> tell you about",
        "two my own tips -> my own two tips",
        "what help me -> that help me",
        "reccommend more communicate -> recommend communicating more",
        "look to life simplie -> look at life simply",
        "In futcher -> In the future",
        "networking give you -> networking gives you",
        "a lot opportunity -> a lot of opportunities",
        "need count -> need to count",
        "try note -> try to note",
        "Delite them -> Delete them",
        "unlacky -> unlucky"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have successfully organized your text with clear structure (Firstly, Secondly) and attempted to use B1-level vocabulary such as 'networking', 'lifestyle', and 'time management'. This shows a good understanding of the task requirements. However, there are frequent spelling and grammatical errors that affect accuracy, particularly with verb patterns (e.g., 'recommend communicate'), prepositions ('look to life'), and spelling ('futcher', 'Delite'). To reach a higher level, focus on proofreading for spelling and reviewing verb collocations. Your ideas are clear and relevant, which is a strong foundation."
}

{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "Hellow -> Hello",
        "me to -> me too",
        "In first semester -> In the first semester",
        "wery -> very",
        "how can I fix -> how I can fix",
        "I will give you them -> I will give them to you",
        "some task-management system -> a task-management system",
        "in uni -> at uni",
        "enouth -> enough",
        "bust -> boost"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have successfully addressed the task with a clear structure and relevant advice. Your use of vocabulary such as 'prioritize', 'task-management system', and 'productivity' is strong and indicates a level above basic. However, there are frequent spelling errors (e.g., 'wery', 'enouth', 'Hellow') and basic grammar mistakes regarding articles, word order, and prepositions (e.g., 'me to', 'in uni', 'give you them'). Improving accuracy in these areas will help you reach a higher level."
}

{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.55,
    "corrections": [
        "It is easily. -> It is easy.",
        "pass all exam -> pass all exams",
        "for became diffiecult study easilier -> to make difficult study easier",
        "It is not obviously tip -> It is not an obvious tip",
        "it really work -> it really works",
        "solve spend -> spend",
        "if you sleep in first iteration -> if you sleep at the beginning",
        "making you eductation esalier -> making your education easier"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have successfully organized your response with clear structure and numbered tips, which demonstrates B1-level text organization. You attempted to explain reasons and use conditional sentences (e.g., 'If you sleep...'). However, there are frequent grammatical errors that affect accuracy, particularly with adjective/adverb usage ('easily' vs 'easy', 'obviously' vs 'obvious'), verb agreement ('work' vs 'works'), and spelling. Focus on reviewing these grammar rules to improve your score."
}

{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.72,
    "corrections": [
        "I think, that -> I think that (Remove comma)",
        "TODO list -> to-do list (Spelling/Formatting)",
        "with the list like this -> with a list like this (Article usage)",
        "Also you can -> Also, you can (Punctuation)",
        "for TODO list -> for a to-do list (Article usage)",
        "into the smaller ones -> into smaller ones (Article usage)",
        "this tips -> these tips (Subject-verb agreement)",
        "would't -> wouldn't (Spelling)"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have written a clear and well-structured response that fulfills the task requirements effectively. Your use of discourse markers (Firstly, Also, But) helps organize your ideas logically, and the advice is practical and easy to understand. The vocabulary is appropriate for the context (e.g., 'productive', 'satisfied', 'concentrate'). However, there are several recurring errors regarding articles ('the' vs 'a'), plural agreement ('this tips'), and punctuation. Paying closer attention to these details will help you move towards a B2 level. Keep up the good work on coherence and task achievement!"
}

{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "has became -> has become",
        "pollutions -> pollution",
        "climate changes is -> climate change is",
        "People who cares -> People who care",
        "It cause -> It causes",
        "making pollution problem looks -> making the pollution problem look",
        "more clear -> cleaner",
        "dumb -> trivial (for formal register)",
        "crazy -> extreme (for formal register)",
        "only people responsible -> only people are responsible"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have successfully followed the task structure by writing a 4-paragraph essay with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion. You attempt to use topic-specific vocabulary (e.g., 'eco-activists', 'politicians', 'environmental health'), which is good. However, there are frequent grammatical errors that affect accuracy, particularly with verb forms ('has became', 'cause', 'cares'), subject-verb agreement ('climate changes is'), and word choice ('pollutions', 'more clear'). Additionally, the register is sometimes too informal for an academic essay (e.g., 'dumb', 'crazy'). To improve to B2, focus on proofreading for basic grammar rules and choosing more formal vocabulary."
}
